name: Maelstrom
primary_sources: Edgar Allan Poe's 'A Descent into the Maelström'

visual_attributes.size_and_scale: up to 10 meters in diameter and 5 meters in depth
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: concentric circles with a central downdraft

domain_specific_visual.water_flow: opposing currents or current running into an obstacle
domain_specific_visual.tidal_influence: often caused by tides in narrow ocean straits

domain_specific_functional.ecological_impact: affects local marine life and watercraft
domain_specific_functional.narrative_role: featured in myths and literature as a powerful force

relationships.description: One of the strongest tidal currents in the world, forming whirlpools.
A system of whirlpools in the Lofoten Islands, Norway.
A narrow strait in Scotland known for its powerful whirlpool.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: global, with notable occurrences in Norway, Scotland, Canada, and Japan
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbolizes danger and mystery in literature and folklore
primary_sources: Edgar Allan Poe's 'A Descent into the Maelström'

contrastive_constraints: NOT a small bathtub or sink whirlpool
NOT a calm or placid body of water
NOT a purely fictional or mythical entity
